title: 2 Terminology
body_text:
2	Terminology
2.1 	Definitions
Branch Number
The branch number is a 5-digit number used to identify the Branch of a Canadian financial institution. The Branch Number and the Institution Number make up the Transit Number.
Chargeback
A chargeback is an item deposited by your financial institution that is subsequently dishonoured by the Drawee financial institution.
Client Centre
The Client Centre is a digital portal that provides secured access to Central 1âs applications and to information and news about our products and services.
Drawee
A drawee is the financial institution to which a debit payment item is addressed. The Drawee is directed to pay the specified amount of money.
Drawee Branch
A drawee branch is the financial institution to which a debit payment item is addressed. The drawee branch is directed to pay the specified amount of money.
Identification Stamp
An identification stamp is a stamp affixed to the back of an item by a financial institution that is involved in the exchange of that item.
Institution Number
The institution number is a 3-digit number assigned by the CPA that identifies a financial institution (e.g., 809 for BC credit unions and 828 for Ontario credit unions). The branch number and the institution number make up the transit number. The institution number is sometimes known as the route number.
Inter-Institution Deposit
An inter-institution deposit is a deposit of items that are returned to another financial institution if dishonoured. This includes the following:
Non-member ATM deposits
Inter-Credit Union (ICU) deposits
Cheques included in Insurance Corporation of British Columbia (ICBC) Deposits
Cheques included in Province of BC Concentrator Account deposits
Cheques included in Receiver General of Canada (Receiver General) Concentrator Account deposits
Location ID
A unique store number assigned by the Province of British Columbia for each BC Services or business office or location.
Sweep
A sweep is the act of aggregating funds from the individual Province of BC accounts held at various financial institutions for the purpose of crediting the Province of BC. Specifically, Central 1 issues a pre-authorized debit (PAD) against each Provincial Concentrator holding account and credits the funds to the Province of BCâs account at Central 1.
Transit Number
The transit number is the Institution Number together with the Branch Number (e.g., 12010-809).
END
